news.Title: Texas Instruments beats Q2 view but shares retreat on elevated expectations
news.Content:
Investing.com -- Texas Instruments Incorporated delivered a sweeping second-quarter beat and provided a third-quarter outlook well above Wall Street estimates, yet its shares dipped more than 5% in premarket trading Thursday.

The paradox highlights an increasingly demanding environment for chipmakers, where robust fundamental performance can trigger profit-taking if market expectations and valuations are already elevated.

The Dallas-based analog semiconductor giant reported second-quarter revenue of $5.46 billion, a 23% increase year-over-year and a 13% sequential gain, topping the consensus estimate of $5.24 billion. Adjusted earnings per share rose 52% to $2.14, handily clearing consensus estimates of $1.92 per share. Revenue was underpinned by strong broad-based demand across industrial, data center, and automotive end markets.

Gross margin for the quarter was 61.4%, above the consensus estimate of 59.4%.

"TI reported a strong quarter and seasonal outlook, basically meeting elevated expectations," Morgan Stanley analysts said in a note. "We view the quarter as broadly in line with elevated expectations given the GM% print and modest guide-up, but with the bar already high, the slight stock pullback was not surprising," they added.

The muted market reaction reflects a classic "sell-the-news" dynamic following a powerful year-to-date rally in the semiconductor sector. With Texas Instruments' shares up sharply entering the print, investors appeared focused on broader macroeconomic headwinds, elevated valuation multiples, and softer areas of the balance sheet, including a 2% contraction in the company's smaller "Other" segment revenue. The stock's pullback underscores how high the bar has been set for semiconductor leaders, even as operational execution remains firm.

"Revenue increased 13% sequentially and 23% from the same quarter a year ago with broad growth led by industrial, data center and automotive," said Haviv Ilan, Chairman, President, and CEO of Texas Instruments.

The company's core Analog division was the primary engine of growth, generating $4.37 billion in revenue, a 26% increase over the prior-year quarter. Operating profit for the segment surged 50% to $1.99 billion. Meanwhile, the Embedded Processing division posted revenue of $788 million, up 16% year-over-year, while nearly doubling its operating profit to $168 million.

Operating cash flow for the trailing 12 months reached $8.67 billion, while free cash flow jumped to $6.53 billion, representing 33.6% of total revenue. Management attributed these strong cash conversion rates to the long-term structural efficiencies gained from expanding its internal 300mm manufacturing capacity. Over the past year, the firm directed $3.9 billion into R&D and SG&A alongside $3.3 billion in capital expenditures, while returning $5.82 billion to shareholders through dividends and repurchases.

Story Continues

Looking ahead, Texas Instruments projected blowout third-quarter revenue between $5.65 billion and $6.15 billion, with the $5.90 billion midpoint comfortably outstripping Wall Street's $5.62 billion consensus. For investors, the focus shifts to whether accelerating demand in data centers and industrial infrastructure can sustain this growth trajectory as macro conditions evolve.

Vahid Karaahmetovic contributed to this report.

news.Title: Nvidia's Grace Blackwell Now Being Built in Texas
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Wistron, the Taiwanese contract manufacturer that assembles AI servers for Nvidia ( NASDAQ:NVDA ), opened its first US plant in Fort Worth, Texas, a $700 million facility that has begun mass production of Nvidia's GB300 Grace Blackwell Ultra Superchip. It is the first of those chips to be built in the United States. Wistron shares closed up 9.70% on the Taiwan Stock Exchange.

The site will also produce Nvidia's next-generation Vera Rubin Superchip, and is designed to assemble and test Nvidia AI systems for American customers, cutting delivery times and strengthening domestic supply chain resilience. Wistron said the plant will use Nvidia's accelerated computing, digital twin technology and AI models to run its own factory operations, and will serve as a hub in its global AI manufacturing network.

Wistron chairman Simon Lin and Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang inaugurated the facility. "The largest infrastructure buildout in history is underway," Huang said, framing the plant as restoring advanced manufacturing capacity in Texas.

news.Title: Archer Aviation Unveils New Commercial Aircraft in Partnership With Anduril
news.Content:
Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR) has spent years developing an electric aircraft designed to carry four passengers across congested cities. Today, July 22, it just unveiled an aircraft that won't carry any humans , not even a pilot.

"Halo" is its name, and it's the commercial sibling of "Thunder," the defense aircraft Anduril unveiled earlier this week. Despite their different missions, Halo and Thunder share the same machinery: a hybrid-electric powertrain and two enormous tilt rotors that lift them vertically from the ground, then turn forward and pull them through the air like conventional airplanes.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

The unveiling is one of Archer's biggest developments of the year. And yet, if you looked only at Archer's stock , you might have thought today was just another ordinary day. As of writing, Archer is down roughly 1%.

Still, I think this is big news for Archer investors, with long-term implications for its business. Let's take a look.

How Halo can help broaden Archer's bull case

In plain English, Halo gives Archer another way to answer a question that has puzzled investors for years: Can the company actually bring a commercially viable product to market (ideally one with wings) before its enormous cash burn devours its financial runway?

It has worked tirelessly to secure FAA type certification for Midnight, its flagship electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft. And although it recently completed the third phase of the FAA's four-phase process, the last step is the most demanding, requiring flight testing and analysis to prove Midnight is airworthy.

On that note, here's a kicker: Archer hasn't yet publicly demonstrated a piloted transition of Midnight (that is, shifting from vertical to forward flight with a pilot in the cockpit). That's not to say Midnight cannot transition. But without a public demonstration, the question will surely linger.

Image source: Archer Aviation.

Speaking of pilots, Halo doesn't need one. Like its sibling, Thunder, the aircraft is autonomous, which could allow it to perform hazardous missions without exposing a crew to danger. Just picture it: Halo can rush medicine to hospitals, or take supplies to disaster zones, or haul machinery to oil rigs. There is, in short, a legitimate commercial need for the kind of autonomous cargo and logistics missions that Halo can pull off. And tapping into that demand could generate meaningful revenue for Archer, something it currently lacks.

Story Continues

But let's not get ahead of ourselves: This is an aircraft, not a revenue reveal. Archer named Marubeni Aerospace as Halo's strategic launch partner, but it hasn't disclosed any firm orders. And unlike Thunder, whose pathway to military airworthiness might be shorter than the FAA certification process for a civilian eVTOL, an autonomous commercial aircraft will likely face a more extensive approval process. Indeed, it's worth noting that Thunder is planned for flight testing in 2027, whereas a first-flight date for Halo was not disclosed in today's announcement.

As such, this is good news, but it still leaves Archer investors in roughly the same place: staring at a potentially large market opportunity that could still be years away.

Does the Archer-Anduril platform make Archer a buy?

The unveiling of Thunder and Halo is a big update from Archer, but it doesn't necessarily make it a screaming buy. Indeed, Archer still faces pretty much the same challenges as before: It needs to certify Midnight, launch commercial operations in the U.S., manufacture aircraft at scale, lay the infrastructure needed to support them, and prove its business can turn a profit over the long haul.

So, is it a buy? If you can tolerate volatility, picking up some shares of Archer now could prove rewarding over time. Most investors, however, will probably want to watch Archer from the sidelines, at least until Midnight secures FAA certification.

news.Title: This Stock Is Crushing Both Lucid and Rivian in 1 Crucial Way
news.Content:
As far as young U.S. electric vehicle (EV) makers go, Lucid Group (NASDAQ: LCID) and Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ: RIVN) have managed to separate themselves from the smaller niche players, or worse, the few that have already closed their doors. One could easily argue that Rivian has even separated itself from Lucid in a positive manner.

But there's another stock, Nio (NYSE: NIO), that often flies under the radar because it was born in China, and in one crucial way it has been crushing Lucid and Rivian recently.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

The bumpy road

It's not an easy life for young EV automakers , which face costly technology such as batteries, largely unprofitable early-stage scaling, and a volatile EV industry that has been impacted by changes in demand due to untimely policy, reduced tax incentives, and even unexpected tariffs.

Despite all of those headwinds, Rivian has taken a large step forward to separate itself in a positive way from rival Lucid in its ability to generate gross profits. These young EV makers being able to generate gross profits , and more importantly, sustainable gross profitability, is a crucial step to proving to investors they can become a viable long-term investment that can one day reward investors.

As you can see, despite starting from a worse position than its rival, Rivian has made consistent progress on gross profitability since the beginning of 2023, while Lucid's gross profitability has languished due to multiple speed bumps.

There are two primary driving forces for Rivian's consistent improvement. One is drastically improved unit economics as the young EV maker has intensely reduced costs, expensive wiring, and the number of parts and sensors, among many other changes. That's expected to continue with the R2, which is targeting about half the costs of the R1.

A second driving force was Rivian's joint venture with Volkswagen , which gave the company the ability to draw non-dilutive capital, split development costs, and sell/license its valuable software stack to its German partner, which has essentially given up on its in-house software division. These software margins are much higher than those for Rivian's hardware manufacturing and helped offset early-stage, less-profitable scaling.

Rivian's improvement has been impressive and consistent, but many investors overlook another EV stock that has taken a leap ahead of even Rivian.

Story Continues

Nio is crushing Rivian?

Nio has witnessed an uptick in its gross profitability, driven by multiple factors, including vehicle deliveries nearly doubling in the first quarter compared to the prior year. Investors have Nio sub-brands Onvo and Firefly to thank for this, as they continue to gain traction.

Going hand in hand with Nio's rise in deliveries are its vehicle and gross margins. During the first quarter, Nio's gross profit topped $700 million, representing a staggering 428.4% increase from the prior year. First-quarter gross margin checked in at 19%, compared to 7.6% during the prior year. Vehicle margin also made a similar jump to 18.8% during the first quarter, compared to 10.2% a year ago.

What it all means

While Rivian has made substantial improvements to boost its gross profitability, it still lacks the growing scale and sales volume that Nio is enjoying. That's the next step for Rivian, and a step it is expected to take with the R2 opening the door to mass-market consumers.

It's natural for U.S.-based retail investors to gravitate toward companies that were founded and operate in the U.S. market, and that's why Rivian and Lucid are more well known than Nio. However, amid the many young EV companies that are struggling globally through many different headwinds and regional speed bumps, Nio has consistently impressed with its ability to navigate a challenging domestic market and a brutal price war, expand its sales and scale, and improve margins and gross profitability.

Rivian has achieved some impressive feats over the past year or two, but when it comes to gross profitability and proving to investors it can be a viable long-term investment, Nio is crushing it -- and investors should take note.

news.Title: Apple rewrites how Americans pay for iPhones
news.Content:
There is a real difference between paying for something and paying to use it. Most people do not notice which one they agreed to until the final payment clears.

For roughly a decade, financing an expensive phone in the United States worked one way. You committed to a monthly number, you made the payments, and at the end the hardware belonged to you.

Apple ( AAPL ) built its own program around that promise. Twenty-four months at zero percent interest, AppleCare+ folded into the payment, an upgrade available after 12 payments, and ownership at the end, according to Apple.

Car buyers know the alternative. A lease lowers the monthly number and hands you nothing at the finish line except the paperwork for your next one.

That structure is now arriving in consumer electronics, and the timing is not an accident. Component costs have pushed Apple hardware prices up faster than most household budgets have moved this year.

The company is preparing to replace its financing lineup with a leasing program called Apple Upgrade, backed by Klarna ( KLAR ), reported TechCrunch .

Why Apple needs a new way to sell its hardware

Apple spent the first half of 2026 raising prices, and it has been unusually blunt about the reason.

The company lifted prices across all Macs, iPads, home devices and the Vision Pro on June 25, reported CNN and TheStreet .

Memory and storage chips that go into laptops now compete for supply with data centers willing to pay far more for the same parts.

Apple said it had "never seen a component price increase this much, this quickly," according to CNBC and TheStreet .

That is the backdrop for a leasing pitch. A lower monthly payment is easier to sell than a higher sticker price, and it moves the conversation away from the number on the shelf tag.

Here is the run-up that made cheaper monthly math look appealing.

The June 25 sweep had no equivalent in Apple's modern history, and the 13-inch MacBook Air moved to $1,299 from $1,099, reported Bloomberg .

Apple shares closed more than 6% lower that session, the worst drop since April 2025, according to CNBC .

IDC analyst Nabila Popal said "the days of $50 price increases are over," reported PBS News .

The iPhone 16, entry-level iPad, Apple Watch SE and MacBook Neo are excluded from the new program, reported Macworld .

Every one of those excluded devices is the cheapest option in its category.

What struck me when I lined up the exclusion list against the price increases is that the program is not built for the budget shopper. It is built to keep buyers one tier above.

Story Continues

Related: Apple's iPhone cost problem reveals AI's hidden bill

What the Apple Upgrade leasing program actually does

Apple Upgrade is scheduled to arrive July 28 in the United States, at retail stores and online, reported TechCrunch . Enrollment requires only a soft credit check.

Terms run 24 months for iPhone and Apple Watch, and 36 months for Mac and iPad.

At the end, a customer can return the device, pay off the remaining value to keep it, or upgrade early. Some of those choices carry extra fees.

Apple will stop new enrollments in the iPhone Upgrade Program and its standard financing options as the new service rolls out, reported 9to5Mac .

More Apple News :

Apple's iPhone 18 Al bet hides a pricing trap

Apple just raised its service prices without telling anyone

Apple's stunning Nvidia flip reveals where Al money is moving

Business and education purchases do not qualify.

When I ran the old terms against the reported new ones, the number that moves is not the monthly payment. It is the AppleCare+ line.

The existing iPhone Upgrade Program bundles AppleCare+ with Theft and Loss into the monthly cost, according to Apple's own program page . The new leases reportedly do not.

Coverage becomes a separate purchase, which means the advertised monthly figure and the actual monthly figure will not match for anyone who wants their phone insured.

That is the tradeoff. You get a smaller headline number and a device you are renting rather than retiring debt on.

The buy now, pay later math working against your budget

Klarna is not a neutral plumbing choice here. The company is one of the largest buy now, pay later lenders in the United States, and that market has been sending warning flares for two years.

Nearly half of buy now, pay later users, 47%, said they paid late on a loan in the past year, up from 41% in 2025 and 34% in 2024, according to LendingTree .

Roughly 29% have used the loans to buy groceries, reported CNBC .

Total buy now, pay later transaction value reached an estimated $70 billion in 2025, or about 1.1% of credit card spending, according to the Richmond Fed .

That is still small next to the card market, and the Richmond Fed found no clear evidence of elevated stress in the sector so far. The concern is behavioral rather than systemic.

Americans are already taking on debt for basics, and installment products make the monthly cost of a purchase feel smaller than the total. Adding a 36-month lease on a laptop to that stack is a real decision, not a checkout formality.

Klarna shares rose 3.9% to $19.50 on the report, though the stock remains down about 33% this year, reported MarketScreener . Keefe Bruyette kept an Outperform rating and a $26 target, according to Benzinga .

What to weigh before your next iPhone purchase

The practical question is simple. Do you keep your phone longer than the lease term?

If you hold a device for four or five years, financing to ownership almost always wins. The payments stop, the phone keeps working, and the money you were sending Apple every month goes back into your budget.

If you replace hardware every year or two, a lease may cost less per month and land close to even over the full term.

The second question is insurance. Price the AppleCare+ coverage separately before comparing monthly figures, because the old program included it and the new one reportedly does not.

The third question is your credit file. FICO said it would begin folding buy now, pay later data into its scores, reported Fortune , and a soft credit check at signup does not tell you how the account gets reported afterward.

Ask that question at the counter before you sign anything.

Apple has not confirmed the program publicly, and a spokesperson declined to comment on the report, reported MarketScreener . That makes July 28 the date to watch.

Klarna reports second-quarter results Aug. 18, which should show whether the Apple relationship is large enough to move the lender's numbers, according to Benzinga .

Then comes the fall iPhone launch, where analysts broadly expect another round of increases. If those land near the $150 to $200 range Counterpoint Research has projected, per CNBC , the monthly payment becomes the only price most buyers will look at.

That is the part worth sitting with. When the sticker price gets uncomfortable, the industry does not usually lower it. It changes what you are buying.

Related: Apple just raised its service prices without telling anyone

This story was originally published by TheStreet on Jul 22, 2026, where it first appeared in the Technology section. Add TheStreet as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

news.Title: Apple Fails to Overturn $634 Million Masimo Verdict
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Apple ( AAPL , Financials ), the consumer technology company behind the iPhone and Apple Watch , failed to overturn a $634 million jury verdict in a patent dispute with medical technology company Masimo.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 8 Warning Sign with GEV.

Is AAPL fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

A federal judge in California rejected Apple's request to set aside the award, which centered on technology used to measure blood oxygen levels in certain Apple Watch models.

The court also found that Apple misappropriated two of 14 Masimo trade secrets. However, the judge declined to issue an injunction and ruled that Masimo could not seek monetary damages on that part of the case.

Masimo had sought as much as $3.1 billion tied to the trade-secret claims. The company, now part of Danaher, has filed an appeal over that portion of the ruling. Apple has argued that its engineers developed the health-monitoring features independently.

The decision keeps pressure on Apple as it continues to defend the technology used in one of its most important wearable products.

Investors will now watch the appeal process and any further impact on Apple Watch features, sales or licensing costs.

news.Title: Here's How Much Berkshire Hathaway's Apple Stake Would Be Worth If Warren Buffett Never Sold a Share
news.Content:
Warren Buffett once joked that outgoing Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) CEO Tim Cook had made more money for Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRKA) (NYSE: BRKB) than Buffett ever did. Of course, it was Buffett's decision to pile about $36 billion into Apple stock between 2016 and 2018, an investment that resulted in massive capital gains for Berkshire shareholders.

Unfortunately, Berkshire has sold more than 75% of its Apple stake since 2018. The stock now trades near its all-time high, about 70% higher than where it was when Buffett started selling the stock in earnest in early 2024. He even joked in an interview earlier this year that he sold it too soon.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

Indeed, if Berkshire had held on to every share it owned in 2018, its stake in Apple would be worth approximately $330 billion today.

Buffett's most profitable investment ever

Despite "selling too early," the Apple investment netted over $100 billion in profit for Berkshire Hathaway, Buffett estimated earlier this year. With the stock price climbing to a new all-time high this month, the value of that investment keeps going up. Apple remains Berkshire's largest equity position.

The reason Buffett sold the stock was simple: The value of Berkshire's stake in the tech company had exceeded the value of everything else in its equity portfolio in 2023.

"I'm very happy to have it be our largest holding," he explained. "I was not happy to have it be as large as almost everything else combined."

While Buffett prefers a relatively concentrated portfolio and favors a strategy of letting his winners run, the portfolio's degree of concentration in Apple became too large even for him. It was all about risk management.

However, 2026 has been another strong year for Apple. The market has rewarded Apple for its relatively capital-light business model compared to other tech giants like Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL). While the hyperscalers are planning to spend hundreds of billions of dollars on capex this year to build and outfit new data centers, Apple's capex has barely budged. It spent about $11 billion on capital expenditures over the past 12 months. That's remarkable for a business that generated over $140 billion in cash from operations over the same period.

Apple has seen strong iPhone sales, and the market still expects strength from its flagship product with potential for an AI-driven upgrade cycle, thanks to the long-awaited Siri revamp released this summer.

Story Continues

Interestingly, Buffett and his successor, Greg Abel, have turned their attention to Alphabet amid its capital spending spree, suggesting the market may have become overly pessimistic about the potential returns on hyperscalers' data center investments. The size of Berkshire Hathaway's bet on Alphabet is quickly approaching the amount of capital Buffett poured into Apple a decade ago.

Buffett said he'd be interested in buying more Apple stock again if the price came down, but the price has only moved higher since he made that comment. Even at today's price, he and Abel appear to be comfortable with Berkshire's current stake in Apple, keeping it as the largest position in the company's stock portfolio for now.

news.Title: TSMC Stock: A $64 Billion Bet Even as AI Bubble Fears Grow
news.Content:
Artificial intelligence (AI) stocks have been on a roller-coaster ride this year. While bulls see AI as a multi-decade tech revolution, bears warn that spending on AI infrastructure has become excessive, which could eventually collapse earnings. Rather than being cautious amid these growing investor concerns, Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM), or TSMC, raised its 2026 capital expenditure budget to between $60 billion and $64 billion in its most recent second-quarter earnings call. TSM stock has climbed 39% year-to-date (YTD), outperforming the overall market's 10% gain so far this year.

It's hard to imagine a company committing that kind of capital while believing AI demand will decline. Why is TSMC spending so much money even as AI bubble fears grow? Let's take a closer look.

More News from Barchart

PayPal Says a $53 Billion Takeover Offer from Stripe Undervalues It. How to Play PYPL Stock Here.

Billionaire Jeff Bezos Called Amazon's Customer Service to Prove a Point But Waited in Silence for More Than 10 Minutes — 'It Was Really Long'

Micron Stock Is Near Bear-Market Territory. Here's Why ASML's Guidance Says Buy the Dip.

Markets move fast. Keep up by reading our FREE midday Barchart Brief newsletter for exclusive charts, analysis, and headlines.

A $64 Billion Investment That Might Worry Investors

TSMC's recent Q2 earnings revealed that demand for advanced chips is not slowing. Total revenue increased 34% year-over-year (YOY) to $40.2 billion, owing to strength in advanced technologies used in AI accelerators, cloud computing, and high-performance computing (HPC) applications. Together, advanced technologies of 7 nanometers and below accounted for an impressive 77% of total wafer revenue.

Segment-wise, HPC accounted for 66% of total revenue and increased 20% sequentially. The performances of other segments like smartphone, IoT, automotive, and digital consumer electronics were mixed. Notably, HPC compensated for weakness in traditional consumer electronics. Despite spending $15.7 billion in capital expenditures in Q2, TSMC saw impressive growth of 77.4% in diluted EPS.

However, the most important announcement that surprised investors was the increase in capital spending. TSMC raised its capital expenditure budget to between $60 billion and $64 billion for 2026. The company plans to spend 70% to 80% of this on advanced process technologies. Additionally, 10% will support specialty technologies, while 10% to 20% "will be spent for advanced packaging, testing, mask making, and others," per CFO Wendell Huang.

Story Continues

Management explained that higher investments have historically resulted in stronger long-term growth opportunities. The statement makes sense. Companies tend to reduce expenditures when there is weaker demand, but there is strong proof of growing demand as TSMC manufactures advanced chips designed by some of the largest tech companies. Its customers include Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), AMD (AMD), Broadcom (AVGO), Qualcomm (QCOM), Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), and Meta Platforms (META). These companies are rapidly expanding their AI infrastructure. Hence, TSMC stands to benefit from that collective growth.

Profitability Faces Near-Term Pressure, But for a Bullish Reason

Much of today's AI investment revolves around GPUs. However, the emergence of agentic AI is creating a revival in CPU demand inside AI data centers, which even Intel's (INTC) management has emphasized. As TSMC manufactures chips for a wide variety of processors, it is well-positioned to win no matter which computing platform gains market share.

TSMC assured that its heavy investments are supported by growing demand. But the company also acknowledged that margins will face temporary pressure because of the same. While Q2 gross margin reached 67.7%, Q3 gross margin is expected to decline to around 66%, with guidance ranging between 65% and 67% due to the rapid production ramp of 2nm technology.

While the lower margin is a short-term concern, investors should note that this isn't due to weak demand or pricing. Instead, it reflects the enormous investment needed to bring the company's newest and most cutting-edge technologies to market, which will support complex AI workloads over the next few years. Management emphasized the same, stating that compared with N2, A14 is expected to deliver 10% to 15% higher performance at the same power, or 25% to 30% lower power consumption at the same speed, along with nearly 20% higher chip density.

TSMC expects Q3 revenue to increase by 37% YOY at the midpoint to land between $44.6 billion and $45.8 billion. Moreover, full-year revenue could grow slightly above 40% YOY. The revenue forecast is further proof that TSMC continues to see extraordinary customer demand across AI infrastructure markets. Analysts also see earnings increasing by 52% in fiscal 2026, with another 28% increase expected in fiscal 2027.

One of the main reasons TSMC can afford such an ambitious investment program is its disciplined cost management and fortress balance sheet. At the end of the quarter, it held $110 billion in cash and marketable securities.

What Does Wall Street Think of TSM Stock?

Every earnings season raises more questions about excessive AI spending as semiconductor firms raise their capex budgets. TSMC's capex budget has raised the same concerns. However, the company also raised its revenue growth target for this year. While temporary margin pressure and rising inventory are making short-term investors skeptical, management believes these are just consequences of bringing new manufacturing technologies online fast enough to satisfy customer demand. TSMC's confidence suggests its next chapter will be considerably stronger than the current one. Long-term investors who can ignore the short-term noise might benefit from holding on to TSM stock.

On Wall Street, TSM stock holds a consensus "Strong Buy" rating. Out of 17 analysts with coverage, 14 have a "Strong Buy," two have a "Moderate Buy" rating, and one offers a "Hold" rating. The average price target of $497.77 implies potential upside of 18% from current levels. Following the strong quarter, Barclays assigned a new high price target of $650 for TSM stock, which suggests potential upside of 54% over the next 12 months.

news.Content:

news.Title: California Hybrids Near First EV Sales Lead Since 2020 as EV Share Hits 17.8%
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Hybrid demand is rapidly reshaping California's auto market as buyers increasingly prioritize affordability, fuel efficiency and practical driving needs. Newly released data from the California New Car Dealers Association showed that hybrids represented almost one in every four vehicles registered in the state during the second quarter, rising from the previous year and comfortably exceeding the 17.8% share recorded by electric vehicles. The strong momentum puts hybrids on pace to overtake annual EV sales in California for the first time since 2020. California is widely viewed as an important indicator for the broader US electric-vehicle market because it is the country's largest plug-in vehicle market and has an extensive charging network.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 6 Warning Signs with TSLA.

Is TSLA fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The changing sales mix could favor automakers with popular hybrid lineups. Toyota Motor Corp. ( NYSE:TM ), whose Camry and RAV4 hybrids ranked among California's top-selling vehicles during the quarter, and Honda Motor Co., whose CR-V also appeared among the state's best sellers, may benefit as more buyers move toward vehicles combining gasoline engines with electric motors. Higher gasoline prices may also be supporting demand, with the war in Iran pushing the national average to $4.02 per gallon as of Tuesday and California's average to $5.52. Jessie Dosanjh, chairman of the California New Car Dealers Association and owner of Stevens Creek Chevrolet, said buyers are currently responding to budget pressures and their individual driving requirements, which is leading many toward hybrids.

Electric vehicles remain under pressure, although the latest figures suggest demand may be beginning to stabilize after a particularly weak first quarter. Zero-emission vehicle registrations in California declined 8.2% from the previous year during the latest quarter, but EV market share improved modestly from the first quarter. Tesla Inc. ( NASDAQ:TSLA ), whose Model Y is an electric vehicle, retained the top-selling vehicle position in the state despite the broader shift toward hybrids. California Governor Gavin Newsom also announced a program with 13 automakers offering first-time zero-emission vehicle buyers or lessees instant rebates of $3,500 for new EVs and $1,750 for used vehicles, potentially providing additional support after President Donald Trump ended the federal tax credit.

news.Title: Arm Gains Ground in AI Servers
news.Content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus .

Arm Holdings ( NASDAQ:ARM ) gained another sign of momentum in the AI race after IDC reported that Arm-based rack-scale GPU servers have overtaken x86 systems as the leading accelerated computing platform.

IDC said global AI infrastructure spending reached $89.7 billion in the first quarter and now expects the market to hit $497 billion in 2026, up nearly 56% from a year earlier. The research firm also forecasts AI infrastructure spending will exceed $1 trillion by 2029.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 4 Warning Signs with INTC.

Is INTC fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Arm licenses processor designs used across smartphones, data centers and AI servers, while x86 chips are primarily supplied by Intel ( NASDAQ:INTC ) and AMD ( NASDAQ:AMD ). IDC said Arm accelerated server spending climbed to $53 billion in the first quarter, while x86 accelerated server spending fell to $34.6 billion as hyperscalers increasingly adopted Nvidia's ( NASDAQ:NVDA ) rack-scale AI systems.

The report also found that enterprises are expanding beyond GPU purchases into CPU-only inference clusters, AI orchestration software and long-delayed storage upgrades, signaling a broader buildout of AI infrastructure.

news.Title: Buy the Dip? Why China's Kimi Model Is Actually Great News for Nvidia.
news.Content: Shares of Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) and most of the AI-related semiconductor sector sold off last week after Moonshot, a China-based AI start-up, released its Kimi 3 model. Kimi made waves across the industry, as the open-weights model displayed impressive performance against even the latest frontier models by Anthropic and OpenAI . Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia investors should not panic over China's Kimi 3 model, as similar past innovations accelerated AI build-out.
• Kimi 3's KDA architecture offers efficiency gains but still requires high-end Nvidia hardware like GB300 NVL72 for optimal performance.
• The model's large parameter count (2.8 trillion) and mixture-of-experts architecture necessitate significant GPU resources and bandwidth.
• Despite potential cost efficiencies, regulatory hurdles and the possibility of Kimi 3 being a distilled US model may limit its adoption.

news.Title: Better Altcoin Buy: XRP vs. Stellar (XLM)
news.Content: Stellar (CRYPTO: XLM) and XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) began as cross-border payment systems created by the same person, Jed McCaleb, but today, they compete in the market for real-world asset tokenization -- financial assets such as Treasuries and stocks that are held on-chain as a tradeable token. And given that the value of tokenized assets will grow from $35 billion today to as much as ...
news.AI_Summary:

• XRP Ledger (XRPL) is positioned better due to Ripple's aggressive institutional buildout, including acquisitions like Ripple Prime and a crypto custody company.

news.Title: Prediction: Micron and Sandisk Stocks Will Both Plummet After July 30
news.Content: The explosive growth of artificial intelligence (AI) has ignited a new supercycle in memory and storage chips. news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: BlackRock draws Wall Street's attention with eye-catching move
news.Content: BlackRock ( BLK ) just stepped into the middle of the AI buildout, and it did so as a lender rather than a builder . The world's largest money manager is looking to raise more than $12 billion in bonds to help fund a Meta Platforms ( META ) data center campus in El Paso, Texas. That single move tells you where BlackRock sees the next decade of fees coming from. For anyone who owns BlackRock stock, or is thinking about it, the deal matters because of how the debt is structured, who carries the risk, and what it signals ...
news.AI_Summary:
• BlackRock is financing a Meta Platforms data center campus in El Paso, Texas, by raising over $12 billion in bonds through a project-specific holding company.
• This structure isolates the debt risk from BlackRock's corporate balance sheet, protecting its credit rating and shareholders.
• Meta Platforms is the primary tenant (20% ownership), with BlackRock funds holding the majority stake (80%) through Global Infrastructure Partners and HPS Investment Partners.
• The deal bolsters BlackRock's fee engine, contributing to its record $15.34 trillion in assets under management and reinforcing its position in private markets and infrastructure financing for AI capacity.
news.Keywords: data center financing, infrastructure investment, asset management, fee income, private markets

news.Title: Analysis-Meta employees' lawsuit shows that if AI fires you, proving it is the hard part
news.Content: By Daniel Wiessner July 22 (Reuters) - A novel lawsuit claiming that Meta Platforms relied on discriminatory AI tools to select employees for layoffs highlights the problems workers face in suing employers over the new technology, including proving how it was actually used. The case helps illustrate why a widely predicted wave of employment lawsuits over AI use has yet ‌to arrive. Legal experts say workers often have little understanding of how AI systems are used in the workplace and many have also signed away their right to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• A lawsuit against Meta alleges discriminatory use of AI in employee layoffs, highlighting challenges for workers proving AI misuse.
• Plaintiffs face difficulties in obtaining evidence of how AI systems were used and are often bound by arbitration agreements.
• Arbitration agreements prevent class-action lawsuits and public jury trials, often favoring employers and keeping disputes confidential.
• The lack of high-profile AI workplace discrimination lawsuits is attributed to these legal hurdles, including proving discriminatory intent and outcomes.
news.Keywords: AI discrimination, employment law, arbitration, litigation risk, workforce impact

news.Title: The Crypto Industry Has Spent Nearly $200 Million to Pass New Pro-Crypto Legislation. Here's What Investors Need to Know.

news.Title: How the Potential $10 Billion Compute Lease Deal Between Meta Platforms and Anthropic Affects Neocloud Stocks
news.Content: Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) has caused some angst for neocloud stockholders. No official announcements have been made, so there are no details yet about how many gigawatts ...
news.AI_Summary:

• Meta is constructing large data centers and plans to build tens of gigawatts of AI infrastructure this decade, potentially becoming a competitor to existing cloud providers.
• The company has a significant compute demand for its social networks, LLMs, and AI initiatives, recently signing a $27 billion compute deal with Nebius.
• Reports suggest Meta could transition from a major customer to a significant seller of AI computing power.
• The potential deal highlights the intense competition and rapid development in the AI cloud infrastructure space.

news.Title: Micron, Western Digital, and Sandisk Just Jumped 12% to 14%. Here's the Forecast That Did It.
news.Content: Tuesday was the day the memory trade turned back around. Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU), Western Digital (NASDAQ: WDC), and Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) rose 12%, 12.5%, and 14.3%, respectively, in Tuesday's session. It was a violent reversal for three stocks that entered the day down 31%, 39%, and 41% from their 52-week highs. The biggest catalyst was a single forecast. In a Monday note, Morgan Stanley reportedly told clients it expects memory prices to rise at least 25% from the second quarter to the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron (MU), Western Digital (WDC), and Sandisk (SNDK) saw significant stock price increases (12-14%) following a Morgan Stanley forecast of a 25% rise in memory prices from Q2 to Q3.
• The forecast attributes the price increase and tight supply to sustained AI data-center demand, with potential for shortages to worsen in 2027-2028.
• Micron reported record Q3 revenue of $41.5 billion (quadrupled YoY) and net income of $28.2 billion, with strong operating cash flow.
• Sandisk's Q3 revenue surged 251% YoY to $5.95 billion, with a non-GAAP gross margin of 78.4%, driven by rising NAND flash prices.
news.Keywords: memory prices, AI demand, supply shortage, DRAM, NAND flash

news.Title: Is Washington Building a Tariff Wall Around American AI?
news.Content: Moonshot AI's July 17 release of Kimi K3 has confronted Washington with an awkward fact: a Chinese open-weight model can approach leading American systems in coding and agentic tasks while charging less, though we challenge the simplicity of that premise here . On July 20, the White House was considering options to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The US government is considering restrictions on American companies' use of Chinese AI models like Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 due to national security concerns.
• Such restrictions could act like tariffs, removing lower-cost competition and preserving a price umbrella for proprietary US models.
• Microsoft (MSFT) has significant exposure through its OpenAI investment and Azure services, while Alphabet (GOOGL) plans a large investment in Anthropic.
• While some hedge funds are reducing Microsoft positions, others are initiating new ones; however, the article suggests other AI stocks may offer higher near-term returns.
news.Keywords: AI models, trade restrictions, national security, cloud computing, market competition

news.Title: Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) Stock Is Down Nearly 15% This Month Despite Record AI Demand. Here Is Why
news.Content: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (NYSE:TSM) reported results on July 16, 2026, triggering a selloff that extended the stock's decline for the month. A Record Quarter, A Falling Stock Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (NYSE:TSM) delivered one of the strongest quarters in its history in July 2026. The stock still lost close to 15% of its value the same month. Second quarter net profit rose 77% year-over-year to a record T$706.6 billion ($22 billion), beating the LSEG consensus estimate of T$632.6 billion ($19.65 billion) and marking its ninth straight quarter ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) reported record Q2 net profit of T$706.6 billion ($22 billion), a 77% YoY increase, and raised its full-year revenue growth outlook to over 40%.
• Despite strong results and demand, TSM's stock fell nearly 15% in July 2026 due to its raised 2026 capex guidance of $60-$64 billion, exceeding market expectations.
• TSM's gross margin of 67.7% beat its own guidance but missed higher buy-side estimates, and Q3 margin guidance was lighter due to two-nanometer node ramp-up costs.
• The broader semiconductor sector, including ASML.AS, also experienced a selloff, with investors becoming more cautious about massive AI spending despite scarce compute capacity.
news.Keywords: semiconductor manufacturing, capital expenditure, AI demand, gross margin, market sentiment

news.Title: Analysis-AI investment boom puts Big Tech's free cash flow under pressure
news.Content: By Patturaja Murugaboopathy and Gaurav Dogra July 22 (Reuters) - U.S. hyperscalers are starting to show returns on their artificial intelligence investments, but the rising cost of the buildout is taking a bite out of their free cash flow, and investors are noticing. At their current trajectory, the so-called "hyperscalers" -- Microsoft, Alphabet, ‌Amazon, Meta Platforms and Oracle -- are expected to spend more combined on capital expenditures than ...
news.AI_Summary:
• U.S. hyperscalers are showing returns on AI investments, but the escalating costs are pressuring free cash flow, causing investor concern.
• By 2027, these companies are projected to spend approximately $340 billion more on capital expenditures than they generate in free cash flow.
• Capex estimates for the five major hyperscalers have significantly increased, reflecting the substantial investment required for AI infrastructure.
• While Microsoft and Amazon show strong revenue growth in AI and cloud, concerns remain about whether cash generation can keep pace with spending.
• Oracle's free cash flow has turned negative, with capex significantly exceeding operating cash flow, leading to a substantial stock decline.
news.Keywords: capital expenditure, free cash flow, AI investment, hyperscaler, operating cash flow

news.Title: Why Upstart Stock Lost 19% in the First Half of 2026
news.Content: Upstart's (NASDAQ: UPST) business has come a long way in recent years. The fintech stock was one of the biggest losers in the post-pandemic bear market as its profits evaporated in 2022, but since then, it has regrouped, returned to generally accepted accounting principles ( GAAP ) profitability, and delivered strong growth. However, that hasn't been enough to please ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Upstart (UPST) stock declined 19% in the first half of 2026 due to concerns about its business model and the departure of CEO Dave Girouard.
• The company reported solid Q4 results with loans originated up 86% to 455,788 and revenue up 35% to $296.1 million, alongside a GAAP profit per share of $0.17.
• However, guidance for 2026 adjusted EBITDA margin was projected to fall slightly, and take rates declined, indicating lower payments from lending partners.
• Despite a widening net loss in Q1, management maintained full-year guidance and projected a 35% CAGR and 28% adjusted EBITDA margin through 2028.
news.Keywords: fintech, AI lending, business model risk, EBITDA margin, loan origination

news.Title: Elon Musk Says the Short Sellers Betting Against SpaceX Have a "Very Low" Survival Probability. Their Bets Now Equal 32% of the Float.
news.Content: "The survival probability of firms who maintain a significant short position in SpaceX over time is very low," SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX) CEO Elon Musk wrote in a post on X last week. That's roughly 32% of the company's publicly tradable shares, and about ...
news.AI_Summary:

• 觀察AI與半導體供應鏈熱度仍佳，台積電獲利持續成長，權值股基本面支撐下，台股大幅下跌空間有限。
• 全球經濟處於高利率、高通膨、低成長環境，但AI新技術帶動產業結構轉型，台灣出口可望受惠雲端服務供應商資本支出擴大。
• 操作策略建議以AI、半導體、產業龍頭及高毛利率族群為優先，留意評價、利率政策與地緣政治風險。
news.Keywords: 台股展望,AI趨勢,半導體供應鏈,產業結構轉型,投資策略

news.Title: Nvidia supplier Wistron launches $700 million Texas factory for AI system production
news.Content: By Wen-Yee Lee TAIPEI, July 22 (Reuters) - Taiwan's Wistron, a supplier to Nvidia, launched a $700 million ‌manufacturing facility in Texas on ...
news.AI_Summary:

• The Fort Worth facility will manufacture Nvidia's GB300 Grace Blackwell Ultra Superchip and the next-generation Vera Rubin Superchip.
• The factory is expected to produce tens of thousands of computing boards per month and has created over 500 jobs, with plans to reach 1,000 employees by year-end.
• This facility is part of Nvidia's $500 billion U.S. investment commitment announced in 2025.

news.Title: Sandisk’s stock buyback program explained

news.Title: Aflac Has Raised Its Dividend for 43 Straight Years. Here's the Catch Income Investors Need to Watch.
news.Content: Aflac (NYSE: AFL) is an insurance company, but it sells what is known as supplemental insurance. These policies are meant to be bought alongside other insurance, paying out only in the event of very specific outcomes, like a cancer diagnosis. However, that isn't the biggest issue you need ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Aflac has a 43-year streak of annual dividend increases, with the latest hike being 5.2%.
• The company generated $4.3 billion in revenue in Q1 2026, with over a third ($1.6 billion) from its Japanese operations.
• Japan accounts for roughly two-thirds of the company's pre-tax adjusted earnings ($759 million vs. $363 million in the U.S.).
• Investors need to monitor the USD/JPY exchange rate as it can materially impact financial results.
• Aflac has a roughly 2% dividend yield and aggressively repurchases its own stock.
news.Keywords: dividend growth, international exposure, currency risk, supplemental insurance, shareholder returns

news.Title: AMD stock gets a new reason to watch from Bank of America
news.Content: Advanced Micro Devices ( AMD ) stock jumped more than 7% Tuesday, July 21, as investors cheered the chipmaker's latest AI infrastructure push and looked ahead to its key AI event. On Monday, July 20, AMD introduced Helios, its first rack-scale AI system, which is expected to begin shipping later this year to customers including Microsoft ( MSFT ), Meta Platforms ( META ), OpenAI, and Oracle ( ORCL ). Helios' rollout is widely considered AMD's biggest step yet into rack-scale AI infrastructure as it ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AMD stock rose over 7% following the introduction of Helios, its first rack-scale AI system, set to ship later this year to major clients like Microsoft and Meta.
• AMD's upcoming AI event (July 22-23) is a key catalyst for updates on its AI roadmap, including the MI450X accelerator and next-gen EPYC processors.
• Bank of America reiterated a 'buy' rating and $620 price target, suggesting AMD could expand its AI infrastructure TAM to over $1.5 trillion by 2030.
• Investors are focused on AMD's ability to compete with Nvidia in rack-scale AI systems and transition into a broader AI systems company.
• Despite a recent 11% pullback in semiconductor stocks, AMD shares are up 152.8% year-to-date, significantly outperforming the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (72.7%) and Nvidia (11%).
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, data center growth, market share, competitive landscape, product roadmap

news.Title: Why Nike Stock Lost 36% in the First Half of 2026
news.Content: Nike (NYSE: NKE) has been struggling for years, and those challenges continued in the first half of the year, pushing the stock lower. Nike told investors that its hoped-for turnaround would take longer than expected; revenue continued to be flat; its CFO said it was stepping down, and tariff-related expenses ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nike stock lost 36% in the first half of 2026 due to ongoing challenges, including a slower-than-expected turnaround.
• Revenue was flat at $11.3 billion in Q3, and gross margin fell 130 basis points to 40.2%, with no expected return to margin growth until Q2 fiscal 2027.
• Tariff-related expenses significantly impacted profits, and the stock fell 15.5% on April 1 following the Q3 earnings report.
• The war in Iran and inflation concerns also weighed on the stock, impacting consumer spending and potentially disrupting supply chains.
• Despite a recent 1% revenue decline in Q4, investors anticipate margin expansion in the upcoming fiscal year, and the stock shows potential upside if growth returns.
news.Keywords: turnaround challenges, margin compression, revenue stagnation, geopolitical risk, consumer discretionary

news.Title: Warren Buffett Keeps 67% of Berkshire's $263 Billion Stock Portfolio in Just 5 Companies
news.Content: Warren Buffett has never believed in owning a little bit of everything. Berkshire Hathaway's (NYSE: BRKA)(NYSE: BRKB) latest 13F filing shows just how far the famed investor leans the other way. Of a $263 billion U.S. stock portfolio, about 67% (more than two-thirds) sits in just five companies. And that concentration isn't a quirk of one quarter. It's how the Oracle of Omaha has run Berkshire's money for decades, and he has been unusually direct about why. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway portfolio is highly concentrated, with 67% of its $263 billion U.S. stock holdings in just five companies.
• The top holdings are Apple ($58B), American Express ($46B), Coca-Cola ($30B), Bank of America ($25B), and Chevron ($17.5B).
• Buffett's strategy emphasizes deep understanding of businesses with durable brands and long dividend records.
• Berkshire also holds significant cash reserves, with $397 billion in cash and Treasury bills at the end of March.
• The lesson for investors is conviction and thorough homework, rather than portfolio diversification for its own sake.
news.Keywords: portfolio concentration, value investing, conviction, durable competitive advantage, capital allocation

news.Title: Here is how much Bitcoin you actually need to get rich like Warren Buffett or Elon Musk

news.AI_Summary:

news.Content:
台股昨（21）日出現報復性V轉，搶短買盤湧現，配合內資法人積極點火、力抗外資賣壓，指數終場勁揚1,783點，創史上最大漲點，收44,232點，一舉收復44K大關及季線失土，技術面日K呈「破底翻」走勢。

回顧台股這波急挫，主要是受韓股連續崩跌拖累，隨韓股止跌反彈，台股攻勢更為猛烈，昨日漲幅4.2%，為最強亞股。展望後市，法人指出，「跌深就是最大利多」，隨台股強彈站回季線，且KD技術指標也低檔呈黃金交叉向上，加上市場氛圍轉多，預期近日行情將可延續彈升走勢，有機會進一步向月線、約45,474點發起挑戰。

三大法人昨日共買超台股162.8億元。其中外資賣超進一步縮減至43.3億元，連13賣、累計賣超6,521.9億元；投信持續買超171.1億元，連20買、累計買超2,209.7億元；自營商買超35億元，轉賣為買。八大公股行庫逢高調節，賣超30.5億元。

台股昨日在台積電（2330）為首的AI權值族群領軍攻堅下，指數開高走高收最高，終場大漲4.2%，成交量則萎縮至8,662億元，為三個多月來最低，呈價漲量縮格局；櫃買指數也大漲3.6%、收381.96點，表現均強勢亮眼。

昨日盤面上個股漲多跌少，上市櫃公司上漲家數達1,487家，下跌家數僅294家；19大類股漲多跌少，尤以電子股勁揚4.8%最強勢；族群中，則以AI、銅箔基板、軍工等表現亮眼突出。

千金股族群一片紅通通，包括穎崴、台光電、創意、聯發科等九檔亮燈漲停，股王信驊大漲逾9%，鴻勁、旺矽、緯穎等也都大漲超過半根停板；檔數因兆聯大漲45元、收1,005元，重回千金股行列，由44檔增加至45檔。

綜合凱基投顧董事長朱晏民、富邦投顧董事長陳奕光、台新投顧總經理黃文清看法，昨日台股多頭強勢發動報復性反擊，展現扭轉本波跌勢的強烈企圖心，美中不足的是量能逆勢萎縮。

倘若多頭能帶量表態突破，反彈走勢將有望進一步向上延伸，進而挑戰頸線關鍵壓力約44,454點。操盤策略上，現階段可針對短線跌深股進行搶反彈的價差交易操作，可優先鎖定具有實質營收成長支撐，且技術面已拉出長紅K棒的個股為首選。

news.Title: Rocket Lab CEO Peter Beck Says Watch the Test Stands. Here's Why the Neutron Timeline Means Everything for Investors.
news.Content: Anyone keeping tabs on orbital-launch service provider Rocket Lab (NASDAQ: RKLB) knows it's working on a company-changing solution. That's its so-called Neutron rocket, capable of lifting up to 28,000 pounds of payload. That's a huge leap from its similarly reusable Electron rocket, with a maximum payload of 660 pounds. This medium-lift portion of the space-launch business that Space Exploration Technologies can also serve is the biggest. Still, Rocket Lab can't afford any further delays in the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Rocket Lab's Neutron rocket development timeline is critical, with delays impacting its competitive position in the medium-lift launch market.
• The company has existing agreements with the U.S. Air Force, NASA, and a satellite operator, but further delays could jeopardize these contracts.
• New competitors like Firefly Aerospace and Relativity Space are emerging with their own medium-lift vehicles, increasing market pressure.
• Rocket Lab's long-term future hinges on successful Neutron launches within the next year, alongside its integrated satellite manufacturing capabilities.
news.Keywords: space launch, rocket development, competitive threats, payload capacity, market entry

news.Title: Nvidia, Micron just got hit by an AI model from Beijing
news.Content: Every market boom eventually runs into the same problem, and it rarely shows up where investors expect it. You ride a simple story — in this case, artificial intelligence needs endless chips and data centers — straight up the chart until the numbers stop feeling real. Then something jolts the narrative, not by breaking the old leaders, but by proving they aren't alone anymore. Right now, that jolt is coming from Beijing. Chinese startup Moonshot AI just introduced Kimi K3, an open‑weight artificial intelligence model built with roughly two point eight trillion parameters, a scale that rivals the largest systems ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Chinese startup Moonshot AI launched Kimi K3, an open-weight AI model rivaling top Western models in performance.
• This development challenges the assumption of a sustained U.S. technological lead in AI and could impact demand for Western chips.
• Nvidia, Micron, and other chipmakers saw their stocks slide as investors digested the implications of cheaper, more accessible high-end AI models from China.
• The PHLX Semiconductor Index fell approximately 10% in the week of Kimi K3's launch, its steepest weekly drop since April 2025.
news.Keywords: AI models, competitive landscape, semiconductor industry, technological parity, market disruption

news.Title: Qualcomm (QCOM) Laps the Stock Market: Here's Why
news.Content: Qualcomm (QCOM) ended the recent trading session at $173.67, demonstrating a +1.97% change from the preceding day's closing price. The stock outpaced the S&P 500's daily gain of 0.89%. On the other hand, the Dow registered a gain of 0.74%, and the technology-centric Nasdaq increased by 1.29%. Heading into ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Qualcomm (QCOM) stock outperformed the S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow with a 1.97% gain.
• However, the company is expected to report a decline in Q4 earnings, with EPS projected at $2.22 (down 19.86% YoY) and revenue at $9.73 billion (down 6.16% YoY).
• Full-year estimates indicate further year-over-year decreases in earnings (-10.31%) and revenue (-3.25%).
• QCOM holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) and trades at a Forward P/E of 15.79, a significant discount to its industry's Forward P/E of 43.32.
news.Keywords: earnings forecast, revenue decline, Zacks Rank, forward P/E, valuation discount

news.Title: Brian Niccol's Starbucks Turnaround Is Quietly Working -- Even With Profit Cut in Half. The July 29 Test Comes Next.
news.Content: Starbucks (NASDAQ: SBUX) earned about half as much in fiscal 2025 as it did the year before. Yet the stock is acting as if the opposite happened. Shares sit near $105 as of this writing, within about 4% of their 52-week high of $109.23. That disconnect is the whole story with this stock right now. The market is paying up for CEO Brian Niccol's turnaround before it fully shows up in profits. And based on the number that ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Starbucks' stock is trading near its 52-week high despite a significant profit cut in fiscal 2025.
• The market is anticipating CEO Brian Niccol's turnaround plan, driven by accelerating customer traffic and comparable sales growth.
• Fiscal Q2 2026 saw global comparable sales rise 6.2% with a 3.8% increase in transactions; U.S. comparable sales jumped 7.1% on 4.3% transaction growth.
• Management raised its full-year outlook, expecting global comparable sales growth of at least 5% and non-GAAP EPS of $2.25-$2.45, but the stock's high P/E ratio (approx. 45) prices in a completed turnaround.
news.Keywords: turnaround strategy, comparable sales, customer traffic, earnings guidance, valuation multiple

news.Title: Prediction: This Dividend ETF's Yield Won't Stay Under 3.5% for Long
news.Content: The Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (NYSEMKT: SCHD) yields about 3.3% right now. That's modestly below its long-term average, and I don't think that will last for long. I expect the yield to climb back above 3.5% within the next year or sooner. Since the beginning of 2023, this exchange-traded fund (ETF) has spent ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD) currently yields about 3.3%, below its long-term average, with expectations to return above 3.5% within a year.
• The ETF's yield dip is attributed to a strong price rally (up nearly 22% YTD), not a reduction in dividend distribution; its first-half 2026 distribution is slightly higher than 2025.
• SCHD has a 14-year streak of consecutive annual dividend increases and has grown its payout by 7.5% annually over the past five years.
• The ETF's ability to steadily generate increasing dividend payments, supported by quality stocks and growth, is expected to drive the yield higher, especially if market conditions shift towards dividends being a larger part of total returns.
news.Keywords: dividend ETF, yield, dividend growth, total return, market outlook

news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology surged 12%, contributing to a 1.29% rise in the Nasdaq Composite.
• Semiconductor stocks, including Sandisk (up 14%) and Advanced Micro Devices (up 8%), experienced a strong rebound.
• The rally in chip stocks was driven by continued AI demand, as evidenced by strong export data from South Korea.

news.Keywords: semiconductor rally, Nasdaq Composite, AI demand, tech stocks, investor sentiment

news.Title: Why Sandisk Shares Are Tumbling and What Investors Should Know
news.Content: Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) has been a breakout success since it went public in February 2025, following its spinoff from Western Digital . The returns for this flash memory storage drive manufacturer for artificial intelligence (AI) accelerators, quite frankly, have been staggering. After its IPO in February 2025, it began trading at $36 per share on Feb. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Sandisk (SNDK) has seen staggering returns since its IPO, with a 3,844% total return and 470% year-to-date return.
• Despite a recent 39% sell-off from its peak, the company's growth trajectory remains strong, with Q3 revenue up 97% sequentially and 251% year-over-year.
• Q4 2026 revenue is anticipated to be between $7.75 billion and $8.25 billion, a 30%-38% increase from Q3.
• Gross margins are projected to increase to 78.9%-80.9% in Q4, and the company has $42 billion in backlog.
• Analysts remain largely positive, with 77% rating it a buy and a median price target suggesting 74% upside.
news.Keywords: flash memory, AI accelerators, revenue growth, gross margin, analyst rating

news.Title: Micron's 29% Drop Has Nothing to Do With Fundamentals
news.Content: Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) stock has shed close to 29% of its value over the past month, but it has nothing to do with fundamentals. The tailwinds of the memory cycle remain intact, and each day further proves that AI demand is rising. The current dip ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) stock has dropped 29% in the past month, but this is attributed to market sentiment rather than fundamentals.
• Demand for AI services, exemplified by Kimi AI's inability to take new customers due to compute limitations, validates the bullish thesis for memory chips.
• Micron's forward P/E ratio has become more attractive, trading at only 5.5, down from approximately 15 at the end of 2025.
• The company's revenue more than quadrupled year-over-year, with profit margins nearing 70% in the most recent quarter.
• The current dip is seen as a compelling buying opportunity, with fundamentals expected to prevail over sentiment.
news.Keywords: memory cycle, AI demand, valuation, forward P/E, buying opportunity

news.Title: Intel makes another painful move in one of its key businesses
news.Content: I have seen this painful chapter before in Intel's Artificial Intelligence ( AI ) story: cutting 40% of your global workforce, halting factories in Germany and Poland, shutting down your automotive chip division, flattening management from 12 layers to six. None of those is comfortable. But it's what radical restructuring looks like when a company is fighting for its industrial life. On July 20, Intel Corporation ( INTC ) added another chapter. The company confirmed it is initiating a new round of layoffs targeting its Data Center and AI Group, DCAI, as part of its broader strategy to become "a ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Intel is initiating another round of layoffs targeting its Data Center and AI Group (DCAI) as part of a strategy to become more focused and efficient.
• This move is counterintuitive as DCAI is Intel's strongest performing segment, with Q1 revenue up 22% year-over-year to $5.1 billion and operating margins at 30.5%.
• The layoffs are driven by competitive pressures from AMD's EPYC processors and the increasing adoption of ARM architecture by cloud providers.
• Intel's CEO is driving a broader transformation, including reducing global headcount by 15% and halting major manufacturing expansions.
• Over 5,000 U.S.-based employees have been laid off, reducing the total global headcount to approximately 81,000.
news.Keywords: layoffs, Data Center and AI Group, competitive pressure, restructuring, operational efficiency

news.Title: Tesla Q2 EPS Preview: Can Earnings Electrify the Stock?
news.Content: Tesla Q2 Earnings Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) stock Tesla ( TSLA ) will report earnings on second quarter earnings results on Wednesday, July 22, after the equity market close. Zacks Consensus Analyst Estimates predict that Tesla will earn $0.50 for Q2, up from the $0.41 the company earning ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla (TSLA) is set to report Q2 earnings, with analysts expecting $0.50 EPS, up from $0.41 in Q1.
• The company has a mixed earnings history, missing estimates in 6 of the past 10 quarters, but has beaten estimates by double digits in the last two quarters.
• Q2 vehicle deliveries exceeded expectations at ~480k, a 25% year-over-year increase, but this may be offset by price cuts and promotional financing.
• The Energy business showed strong growth with deployments up 40% year-over-year, and a new pact with SunRun was announced.
• Investors will be looking for progress on future products like Robotaxi, FSD, and Optimus, and clarity on whether strong delivery volumes can offset margin pressures.
news.Keywords: Q2 earnings, EPS, vehicle deliveries, margins, energy business

news.Title: Berkshire Hathaway's New Leader Has Loaded Up on Alphabet With $10 Billion in Cash. Here's the Likely Reason.
news.Content: At the end of last year, Warren Buffett retired as chairman and CEO of Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRKA)(NYSE: BRKB) and passed the reins to Greg Abel. Although Buffett still has some influence on the company, it's clear that Abel and Berkshire Hathaway are embracing new strategies. Since Abel took over, Berkshire Hathaway's stock portfolio has ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Berkshire Hathaway has significantly increased its stake in Alphabet (GOOGL), making it its fifth-largest holding with an 8.7% portfolio allocation.
• The total stake, including a new $10 billion private placement, is nearly $31 billion, reflecting a strategic shift under new leadership.
• Alphabet is viewed as a full-stack AI company with operations in research (DeepMind), hardware (AI chips), infrastructure (Google Cloud), and applications (Gemini).
• Google Cloud is a rapidly growing segment, with Q1 revenue jumping 63% year-over-year to $20 billion, outpacing AWS and Azure growth rates.
• Alphabet is considered a stable way to invest in AI, offering strong cash flow and a dominant position in online search, reducing reliance on third-party hardware providers like Nvidia.
news.Keywords: Alphabet, Berkshire Hathaway, AI investment, Google Cloud, vertical integration

The Magnificent 7 may not even be a "thing" anymore, but these two companies are still among the most widely held stocks. Investors and traders will be watching their earnings reports closely. In addition to ...
news.AI_Summary:

• Intel (INTC) has beaten earnings for three consecutive quarters, with significant expected earnings growth of 154.8% this year and 40.2% next year, trading at a forward P/E of 91.

news.Keywords: earnings season, Alphabet, Tesla, Intel, AI Revolution

news.Title: These 2 Analysts Just Upped Their AMD Stock Price Targets. Here’s Why.
news.Content: Wall Street has not been shy about backing Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) lately, and two prominent analysts have just added even more fuel to the fire. Rosenblatt analyst Kevin Cassidy raised his price target on AMD stock ahead of the company's Advancing Artificial Intelligence (AI) event on Wednesday, July 22 and Thursday, July 23, calling it his firm's top semiconductor pick. Cassidy believes Advanced Micro's momentum has plenty of room to run. He expects revenue from Extended Performance Yield Computing (EPYC) server CPUs to surge more than 70% year-over-year (YOY) while generating margins comfortably above the company average. More News ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Two analysts have raised their price targets for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), with Rosenblatt calling it their top semiconductor pick.
• AMD's EPYC server CPU revenue is expected to surge over 70% year-over-year with strong margins, and the upcoming Venice chip is anticipated to perform well.
• UBS expects AMD's AI Day to reveal stronger CPU/GPU roadmaps, new AI partnerships, and an increasing list of data center customers.
• Microsoft will deploy AMD's Helios AI platform across Azure cloud services, with Helios also scheduled for Meta and Oracle.
• AMD's revenue climbed 37.8% year-over-year to $10.3 billion in Q1 FY2026, beating analyst expectations.
news.Keywords: AMD, EPYC, AI Day, data center, semiconductor

news.Title: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Owns Zero Pure-Play AI Stocks. But This 2016 Acquisition Gives It Exposure to the Data Center Boom.
news.Content: Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRKA) (NYSE: BRKB) is not as technology-shy as it once was. Over the past few decades, the holding company, led by Warren Buffett until his retirement in 2025, has increased its exposure to tech stocks . Currently, this includes not just its large position in Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL), but also a burgeoning position in ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Berkshire Hathaway has increased its exposure to tech stocks, including a burgeoning position in Alphabet (GOOGL), which is considered an AI play.
• While not a 'pure-play' AI stock, Berkshire gains AI exposure through its 2016 acquisition of Precision Castparts.
• Precision Castparts, which makes specialty metal components, is benefiting from the AI data center boom as hyperscalers use gas-powered turbines that require similar components.
• Precision Castparts' annual operating cash flow increased to $2.4 billion last year, up from $900 million in 2021.
• Berkshire's strategy of acquiring high-quality assets at fair prices positions it to benefit from emerging trends like the data center boom.
news.Keywords: Berkshire Hathaway, Alphabet, AI exposure, data center, Precision Castparts

news.Title: Why Redwire Stock Popped on Tuesday
news.Content: Redwire Corporation (NYSE: RDW) stock investors had an amazing day Tuesday, with their stock closing up 9.4% on a couple of announcements suggesting Redwire has momentum: Yesterday, Redwire announced a "major expansion" of ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Redwire Corporation (RDW) stock rose 9.4% on announcements of factory expansions for drone and microgravity payload production.
• The company is expanding its Huntsville, Ala., factory for Stalker UAS armed surveillance drones and opening a second factory in Georgetown, Ind., for microgravity payloads.
• Redwire has pivoted into the defense sphere, acquiring drone company Edge Autonomy for $925 million and integrating AI-enhanced military drones.
• The Georgetown facility will focus on pharmaceutical/biotech innovation in Low-Earth Orbit and on the moon.
• A key risk to watch is the cost of expansion, as Redwire reported losses of $300 million over the last 12 months and is burning cash at approximately $155 million per year.
news.Keywords: Redwire, drone production, microgravity payloads, defense, factory expansion

news.Title: This Is My Favorite Artificial Intelligence Stock to Buy Right Now (Hint: Not Nvidia, Broadcom, or Alphabet)
news.Content: Many top artificial intelligence (AI) stocks have underwhelmed recently. Palantir Technologies stock, for example, is down 25% so far this year, while semiconductor king Nvidia is slightly underperforming the S&P 500 . Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE: TSM) stock, though, is outperforming the market, up 32% year to date. Here's why it's my favorite AI stock to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) is highlighted as a favorite AI stock due to its dominant market share (73%) and significant investments.
• TSMC announced an additional $100 billion investment in its Arizona foundry, planning for a total of five facilities there and more in other U.S. locations.
• The company's diversified revenue base serves all major tech giants, including those using various CPU architectures, and extends beyond AI to smartphones and autonomous vehicles.
• Despite a recent stock dip possibly due to AI infrastructure spending concerns, TSMC is well-positioned to power future technology advancements.
• TSMC's management emphasizes its competitive advantages in technology, manufacturing, and customer trust.
news.Keywords: semiconductor foundry, market share, capital investment, diversified revenue, technology leadership

news.Title: Meta Just Got a Major Boost From Zuckerberg, and Investors Should Take Notice
news.Content: Due to Meta Platforms ' (NASDAQ: META) plans to spend massive sums on artificial intelligence infrastructure, its shares have fallen substantially from the 52-week high of $796.25 they reached last August. But the stock's trajectory has changed in recent days, edging up past $600. The catalyst for Wall Street's renewed optimism is Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's plan to turn the company's expensive ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms (META) is planning to leverage its substantial AI infrastructure investment by launching a cloud computing business to sell access to its AI models.
• This strategic shift aims to create a new revenue stream and diversify Meta's business beyond its advertising-dependent model.
• Meta plans to spend up to $145 billion on capital expenditures this year, significantly increasing from $72.2 billion in 2025.
• Unlike the metaverse, AI has broad market traction, and Meta's vision includes developing personal superintelligence accessible to everyone.
• Meta's stock, trading at a forward P/E ratio of 21, is considered attractively valued, suggesting a potential buying opportunity.
news.Keywords: AI cloud, revenue diversification, capital expenditure, artificial intelligence, market opportunity

news.Title: Is Intuitive Surgical a Buy After Its Latest Earnings Report?
news.Content: Intuitive Surgical (NASDAQ: ISRG) delivered its second-quarter earnings report last week. It crushed the average analyst bottom-line estimate for profitability and edged past the consensus for revenue. Why, then, was the stock punished by a concentrated investor sell-off that it hasn't yet recovered from? Here's a look at the quarter and -- more importantly -- whether the company's weakened share price makes it an irresistible bargain. Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) reported Q2 earnings that exceeded analyst expectations for revenue and profitability, with total revenue up 19% year-over-year.
• Procedure volume increased by approximately 16%, with da Vinci procedures up 15% and Ion procedures up 36%.
• Net income (non-GAAP) rose nearly 26% year-over-year to over $1 billion, or $2.80 per share.
• Despite strong fundamentals, the stock experienced a sell-off, likely due to a slowdown in da Vinci procedure growth in the U.S. market.
• Management maintained full-year guidance for da Vinci procedure growth and adjusted gross profit margin, but noted potential pressure from scrutiny of healthcare expenses in China.
news.Keywords: earnings report, revenue growth, procedure volume, market slowdown, guidance
